description: §61.4 — an `<endpoint>` whose arms are NOT exhaustive against the `accepts=` enum (the `DeltaSince` variant has no arm) fires E-ENDPOINT-NOT-EXHAUSTIVE. This is the inbound-honesty guarantee: because scrml owns the decode, the unhandled-variant hole is visible, so it is a compile error, not a silent runtime 404/500.
rationale: §61.4: the arms SHALL be exhaustive against the `accepts=` enum (reusing the §18.0.1/§51 exhaustiveness machinery). A variant with no arm (and no wildcard) is E-ENDPOINT-NOT-EXHAUSTIVE (W3 typer).
